title: Testing Assessment
executiveSummary: SnapBack has a comprehensive testing infrastructure with unit, integration, performance, security, and end-to-end tests across multiple packages. The testing framework is built on Vitest with good code coverage configurations. Tests are organized by type and include mocking strategies for external dependencies. The codebase demonstrates strong testing practices with specific focus on security validation and performance budgets.

testingInfrastructure.configuration: Per-package vitest.config.ts files with centralized root configuration

unitTests.description: Focused on testing individual functions and components in isolation

integrationTests.description: Test interactions between multiple components and services

performanceTests.description: Validate performance budgets and response times

securityTests.description: Validate security controls and boundary checks

endToEndTests.description: Test complete workflows and user journeys

mocking.description: Extensive use of mocking for external dependencies
examples: Mock MCP SDK in performance tests

testData.description: Use of fixtures and test data generators

positiveAspects: Good use of mocking for external dependencies
areasForImprovement: Test data management could be more centralized

specificStrengths.description: Dedicated security tests for path validation and boundary checks
Explicit performance budget validation with time constraints
Tests organized by type with specific directories for each test category
examples: Unit, integration, performance, security, and e2e test directories
Specific test files for different aspects of functionality

findings.description: Comprehensive testing infrastructure with multiple test types
findings.details: The codebase includes unit, integration, performance, security, and end-to-end tests with appropriate organization

findings.description: Strong focus on security testing with dedicated test files
findings.details: Path validation and boundary checking are thoroughly tested with various attack scenarios

findings.description: Performance budget validation with explicit time constraints
findings.details: Performance tests validate specific response time budgets for critical operations
Some end-to-end tests contain placeholder implementations rather than actual validation
Test strategies and patterns are not well documented for new team members

recommendations.description: Implement actual validation in placeholder tests
recommendations.details: Replace placeholder implementations in end-to-end tests with actual validation logic

recommendations.description: Add documentation for test strategies and patterns
recommendations.details: Create documentation explaining the testing patterns and strategies used in the codebase
Create a centralized test data management system for consistent test data across packages
Make coverage reports more easily accessible through CI/CD pipelines or documentation
